strategy.name: Tone Vays — Multi-Timeframe Exhaustion & RSI Divergence
strategy.description: Derived from Tone Vays' Q&A sessions and trading videos. Core logic: wait for trend exhaustion on the 4H chart (RSI(3) deeply oversold in an uptrend, or deeply overbought in a downtrend), confirmed by price position relative to VWAP and EMA(8). Only trade when all three layers agree. Conservative sizing — never risk more than 1% per trade. Tone Vays' actual system uses DeMark Sequential (MRI buy/sell signals); RSI(3) is used here as the closest computable proxy for that exhaustion signal.

indicators.ema_8: Trend direction on the 4H chart. Price above EMA(8) = momentum is up. Below = momentum is down. Tone Vays uses a declining moving average as a resistance signal — if price is below a declining EMA, the trend is bearish.
indicators.vwap: Session bias. Price above VWAP = institutional buyers in control. Below = sellers. Tone Vays looks for price to be on the right side of key averages before entering.
indicators.rsi_3: Exhaustion signal. RSI(3) is the computable proxy for Tone Vays' DeMark Sequential (MRI buy/sell). When RSI(3) < 20 in an uptrend, short-term sellers are exhausted — snap-back likely. When RSI(3) > 80 in a downtrend, buyers are exhausted — reversal likely. Tone Vays waits for exhaustion to show on both weekly AND daily — we apply the same principle within the 4H chart.

bullish: Price is above VWAP — buyers in control for the session
Price is above EMA(8) — 4H uptrend intact
bearish: Price is below VWAP — sellers in control
Price is below EMA(8) — 4H downtrend intact
neutral: Price is above one but not both — no clear bias, no trade

long: Price is above VWAP — session bias is bullish
Price is above EMA(8) — 4H trend is up
RSI(3) has dropped below 20 — exhaustion signal, snap-back setup (Tone Vays' MRI buy proxy)
Price is within 1.5% of VWAP — not overextended, risk/reward is acceptable
short: Price is below VWAP — session bias is bearish
Price is below EMA(8) — 4H trend is down
RSI(3) has risen above 80 — exhaustion signal, reversal setup (Tone Vays' MRI sell proxy)
Price is within 1.5% of VWAP — not overextended, risk/reward is acceptable

exit_rules: Exit long when RSI(3) crosses back above 60 or price touches EMA(8) from below
Exit short when RSI(3) drops back below 40 or price touches EMA(8) from above
Hard stop loss: 1.5% below entry for longs, 1.5% above entry for shorts (Tone Vays always defines stop before entry)
Raise stop to breakeven once trade moves 1% in your favour — Tone Vays principle: 'no risk trades'
risk_rules: Maximum 1% of portfolio at risk per trade — Tone Vays never risks more than this
Maximum trade size capped by MAX_TRADE_SIZE_USD in .env
Maximum trades per day capped by MAX_TRADES_PER_DAY in .env
Do not trade if price is more than 1.5% away from VWAP — Tone Vays avoids chasing overextended moves
Do not trade if there is no clear weekly/daily alignment — when in doubt, stay out (core Tone Vays principle)
Paper trade first — Tone Vays always validates a strategy before risking real capital
notes: Tone Vays' actual system relies on Tom DeMark's proprietary Sequential indicator ('MRI buy/sell signals'). That indicator cannot be computed here without a TradingView connection. RSI(3) below 20 / above 80 is the closest measurable proxy for DeMark exhaustion signals. The philosophy — wait for trend exhaustion, confirm bias on multiple timeframes, use tight stops, never chase — is faithfully preserved. Timeframe: 4H. Asset: XBTUSDT on Kraken.
